tiny_practice.action: Close your eyes and imagine God's incredible promise of establishing David's eternal kingdom. Breathe deeply and reflect on how God's plans exceed human expectations.

two_step.reflect_question: How do you respond when God's plans are different from your own initial intentions?

options.text: Surrender my plans and trust God's direction
options.response: Pray and seek God's wisdom before making major decisions

options.text: Resist and try to force my original plan
options.response: Journal about areas where you need to release control

options.text: Wait patiently for God to reveal His purpose
options.response: Practice listening prayer and biblical meditation

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a picture of a house or kingdom that represents God's eternal promises. Include symbols of stability, protection, and love.

reevaluation.question: How has reading this chapter changed your understanding of God's promises?
options: God's plans are bigger than my immediate circumstances
Divine promises are not always fulfilled immediately
God values relationship over religious performance
Humility opens the door to unexpected blessings

three_choice.question: Which theological perspective resonates most deeply?

choices.expansion: Divine selection transcends human planning or ambition
God's commitments extend beyond individual lifetimes into generational promises

choices.text: Human plans must submit to divine will
choices.expansion: Human desires are secondary to God's ultimate redemptive strategy

socratic_nudge.question: If God declines your most passionate project, might the decline itself be part of a greater blessing?

moral_slider.question: How do human aspirations align with divine purposes?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of divine guidance shifted?
options: God's perspective transcends my immediate desires

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Covenant with David
thematic_summaries.summary: God establishes an unconditional covenant with King David, promising an eternal kingdom through his descendants. This covenant reveals God's redemptive plan and foreshadows the messianic lineage leading to Jesus Christ.

thematic_summaries.title: God's Sovereignty in Divine Plans
thematic_summaries.summary: God redirects David's human intention of building a temple by establishing His own divine purpose. This demonstrates that divine plans supersede human strategies and that God's wisdom transcends human understanding.

reevaluation.question: How does God's covenant with David challenge traditional understanding of divine promises?
options: The covenant demonstrates God's commitment transcends human limitations
It reveals the prophetic lineage leading to the Messiah
It shows divine promises are not dependent on human worthiness
The covenant represents a paradigm shift in understanding redemptive history
